entry.title: Production Order Confirmation Posts Successfully but GI 261 Is Not Created via EML.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

We are using EML on I_ProductionOrdConfirmationTP to post a production order confirmation together with goods movement data through the child entity _ProdnOrdConfMatlDocItm. The confirmation is created successfully, but the 261 Goods Issue (MIGO equivalent) is not posted, and no backend error is returned.

Has anyone faced this situation where the confirmation BO processes correctly but does not generate the expected GI 261? Is there any specific prerequisite or RAP BO limitation that prevents the 261 movement from being created in this scenario?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Change description of Special GL Indicator
entry.content:
Dear @Aish16,

In Public Cloud you cannot change or create Special G/L indicators, which also means you cannot change the description/text of indicator C from Value-Dated Bank Transfer to Interest Payable. SAP explicitly states that Special G/L indicators are pre-defined by SAP in Public Cloud

SAP Note and SAP Community below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2761212

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/special-general-ledger-indicators-and-general-ledger-account-transactions/qaq-p/12324056

What SAP does allow in Public Cloud is only the related account determination:

  • In CBC ID 100297 Automatic Account Determination you can define alternative reconciliation accounts for Special G/L transactions but this does not change the indicator description shown in value helps / UI

 

So to your backend question: No, there is no supported backend/config/table way in Public Cloud to rename Special G/L indicator texts

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Webinar Announcement: AI-Powered Resume Assessment - The Future of Workforce Screening
entry.content:
In today's competitive talent landscape, the difference between finding exceptional candidates and settling for mediocre ones often comes down to one critical factor: how effectively you can screen and assess resumes at scale. Traditional manual resume review processes are not only time-consuming and inconsistent, but they also introduce unconscious bias and often miss qualified candidates buried in high-volume applicant pools. The solution? AI-powered resume assessment—a transformative capability that's revolutionizing how organizations identify, evaluate, and prioritize top talent through intelligent, unbiased screening processes.

We're excited to introduce our upcoming lab session, AI-Powered Resume Assessment: Why It's a Must-Have for Every Program - a comprehensive, practical workshop designed for SAP Fieldglass users ready to modernize their screening processes and unlock measurable efficiency gains. Whether you're exploring AI adoption for the first time or looking to optimize your existing workforce management workflows, this session provides the complete A-to-Z walkthrough you need to implement AI-powered resume assessment with confidence and immediate impact.

Why You Should Attend

1. Expert-Led Implementation Guidance

Learn from seasoned SAP Fieldglass specialists who have successfully deployed AI-powered resume assessment across diverse industries and program types. Gain access to proven configuration strategies, implementation best practices, and insider tips that can accelerate your deployment timeline while avoiding common pitfalls that slow down adoption.

2. Measurable Efficiency and Quality Gains

Discover how AI-driven scoring brings consistency and fairness to your candidate evaluation process, eliminating format variations and subjective inconsistencies that plague manual review. Understand the tangible impact on screening speed, quality improvements, and bias reduction that organizations are achieving, with real metrics and benchmarks you can apply to your own program.

What's on the Agenda

  • Introduction to AI-Powered Resume Assessment in SAP Fieldglass
  • Complete Configuration Walkthrough: From Setup to Go-Live
  • Measuring Success: KPIs, Metrics, and ROI Demonstration
  • Real-World Case Studies: Efficiency Gains and Quality Improvements
  • Interactive Q&A Session with Implementation Experts

Who Should Attend

This lab is ideal for talent acquisition leaders, procurement managers responsible for external workforce programs, SAP Fieldglass administrators, and HR technology specialists. It's particularly valuable for organizations with high-volume staffing needs, multiple supplier relationships, or teams seeking to reduce manual screening overhead while improving candidate quality and consistency.

1. Organizations Beginning Their AI Journey

Teams exploring AI capabilities but uncertain about implementation complexity will discover a low-risk, high-impact entry point. Learn how to adopt AI-powered resume assessment with minimal technical overhead while achieving immediate efficiency improvements that demonstrate clear ROI.

2. High-Volume or Multi-Supplier Programs

Organizations managing large numbers of suppliers or multiple MSPs will see how AI-driven scoring creates consistency across varied resume formats and submission quality. Eliminate the inefficiencies of manual review while ensuring fair, unbiased evaluation of all candidates regardless of source.

3. Fast-Growing External Workforce Programs

Programs dealing with increasing requisition volume, time-sensitive staffing needs, or resource constraints will discover how AI assessment reduces manual review time while improving screening accuracy. Transform bottlenecks into competitive advantages through intelligent automation.

How to Register?

Secure your spot in this essential hands-on lab session by registering at the link below. This interactive workshop is designed to provide immediate, actionable value that you can implement right away.

Registration Link: https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5234455/09D6931769E691DAE81020D168F92A74

Event Details

Date: March 18, 2026
Time: 10 AM ET / 2 PM GMT / 3 PM CET / 6 PM GST
Format: Interactive hands-on lab with live Q&A
Recording: Available on-demand following the live session
Note: APAC-friendly replay session planned for April

Investment in Future

In an era where talent acquisition speed and quality directly impact business outcomes, AI-powered resume assessment isn't just a nice-to-have feature—it's a strategic necessity. This lab session represents an investment in your organization's competitive advantage, providing the knowledge and tools needed to transform your screening processes and deliver measurable value to stakeholders.

Call to Action

Don't let manual screening processes limit your program's potential. Join us for this comprehensive lab session and discover how AI-powered resume assessment can revolutionize your talent identification process. With hands-on guidance, proven best practices, and immediate implementation support, you'll leave this session ready to drive measurable improvements in efficiency, quality, and candidate experience. Register today and take the first step toward modernizing your workforce management approach.

entry.title: Widget to show a feed containing more than 15 items
entry.content:
I'm using a Workzone content widget to display blog posts contained in a specified content folder. Great.

The max number of items it can display is 15. This means posts that drop out of the top 15 simply aren't displayed and they're lost to users. The widget doesn't have paging controls, which seems like a bit of an oversight. How are colleagues supposed to find older articles?

I've had a look through the other widgets and I can't find anything that will serve as a long-term space for blog type articles. 

What's the solution here?

entry.title: Re: Crossboarding process (internal hire process) should trigger from Recruitment Management Only
entry.content:
This article whilst helpful, needs further refinement.  Once the record is saved and you make another update in EC you need to build a mechanism to strip out the data otherwise crossboarding will be triggered from EC.

Personally if you want to build this logic then set a different event reason from RCM for a transfer than the one in EC that is triggered by ERD.

Margit

entry.title: GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm excited to share the update regarding our latest release - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 for GRC, Trade, and Tax. This update highlights many significant innovations from SAP Risk and Assurance Management, SAP Watch List Screening, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

For a quick overview of the top release highlights for GRC with 2602, you can watch the video below.

Video 1. Release Highlight Video for GRC in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602

This blog covers the following innovations:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)
  • Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments
  • E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities
  • Enabling Electronic Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 for Poland​

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

  • Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

  • Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations
  • Management-focused monitoring dashboard

SAP International Trade

  • Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

​SAP Watchlist Screening

  • Delta Screening​

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Tax accountants often spend significant time validating data by comparing current tax figures with previous returns. To accelerate this process, we introduce AI-assisted trend analysis for VAT returns. This feature helps tax experts by automatically uncovering hidden trends, flagging outliers, and detecting root causes for discrepancies. It provides intelligent summaries that can be used to simplify the approval process and support future audits. This innovation allows the team to move from reactive validation to proactive analysis.

Picture 1. AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Value Proposition​
  • Reduce the time needed to interpret VAT data across periods, leading to faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Accuracy is improved and compliance risk is reduced. This helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings.
  • Easy to make clear and informative data-driven decisions.
Capabilities
  • The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that help to identify potential errors or compliance risks. ​
  • Provide concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.​

Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

During the preparation of statutory reports, tax experts require deep visibility into their data to ensure accuracy. With the 2602 release, the 'Manage Tax Items' activity within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is enhanced with a powerful ''analyze data" option.

This provides tax experts with additional fields and the flexibility to analyze tax data to identify anomalies. Now you can easily see how invoices are mapped to your VAT returns and pinpoint any documents that might have been excluded, enabling seamless adjustments integrated directly with accounting. To further tailor the solution, you can now use extensibility to plug in your own custom analytics CDS views, allowing you to choose between standard SAP-delivered queries or your own defined views for analysis.

 

Video 2. Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

Value Proposition​
  • Advances the VAT review and adjustment by integrating analytics.
  • Improves accuracy and strengthens compliance through data-driven decisions.
  • Reduces manual effort and reliance on offline analysis and external tools​.
  • Enhances analytical transparency and enables faster and more confident decision-making.​
Capabilities ​
  • Embedded analytics within the Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting application​
  • Direct access to detailed VAT tax line-item records with added data points​
  • Interactive analytical views with charts, filters, and drill-down capabilities​
  • Trend analysis and comparisons across multiple VAT dimensions​
  • Identification of documents for inclusion in or exclusion from VAT returns​
  • Analysis at the point of action without switching applications​
  • Support for efficient VAT reconciliation and compliance preparation​

For more information, see: Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting | SAP Help Portal

E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

A growing number of tax authorities are providing pre-populated, or draft, tax returns based on real-time transactional data. Our latest update helps you automate the handling of these draft returns, ensuring consistency with your company's books. With this feature, you can pull auto-populated returns from an authority's portal and automatically compare them with the returns prepared in your ERP system to identify any mismatches.

Based on the reconciliation results, you can then process actions such as submitting a delta file, accepting, or rejecting the draft. This streamlines the entire process, from review and correction to the submission of the final return.

 

Video 3. E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

With the Polish Ministry of Finance enforcing the use of electronic invoices through the KSeF 2.0 platform beginning in February 2026, we are equipping you to meet this mandate head-on. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance now enables you to generate B2B e-invoices in the required XML format from both Finance (FI) and Sales & Distribution (SD), including tax invoices, correction invoices, down-payment invoices, and self-billing invoices. You can submit these invoices interactively to KSeF 2.0, visualize them in a human-readable PDF format with a QR code, and retrieve supplier invoices for automated posting.

Picture 2. Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

We are introducing the new Risk and Compliance Cockpit, an SAP Analytics Cloud story that gives you an instant, comprehensive overview of your organization's compliance landscape.

This dashboard empowers compliance managers to identify process inefficiencies by visualizing compliance issues organized by type and status, with charts showing trends over time. It makes it easy to compare current performance against historical data, highlight long-term trends, and identify deviations. With insights into average issue resolution times and year-to-year analysis, this single source of truth helps you prioritize critical unresolved issues and improve operational efficiency.

 

Video 4.  Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

Value Proposition​
  • Get an instant overview of compliance issues, organized by type and status​
  • Improved operational efficiency supported by trend and year-to-year analysis​
  • Charts visualize trends over time​
  • Overview of what to prioritize by highlighting critical unresolved issues​
​Capabilities ​
  • Identify inefficiencies in organizations' processes​
  • Instant overview of compliance issues​
  • Filter business data to examine specific categories of compliance issues​
  • Visual and comparative analysis of data​
  • Compare current performance while highlighting long-term trends and deviations

For more information, see: Risk and Compliance Cockpit

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

In SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, investigations are the central hub for examining security incidents. This new capability automates and enhances this critical process.

The primary value of this feature is to decrease the manual effort required by SAP monitoring agents and increase their overall efficiency. When an agent prepares an investigation to send to a customer, they are now supported with automatic, case-specific response and mitigation recommendations.

This helps teams by automatically compiling investigation results and formulating suggestions for customers on how to address the alerts. The system intelligently generates these recommendations based on the standard operating procedure of the specific pattern that created the alert, further enhanced by predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. This ensures that the advice provided is both highly relevant and immediately actionable, allowing your security teams to move from detection to resolution faster and more effectively.

Picture 3. Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

Management-focused monitoring dashboard

We are introducing a powerful new monitoring dashboard within SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, specifically designed for management roles. In today's complex security landscape, having a clear, high-level view of your organization's cyberhealth is not just an advantage; it is a necessity. This new dashboard is for all managers and security leaders who need to quickly understand current security events, alerts, and ongoing investigations to make informed, timely decisions.

It facilitates a deeper understanding of complex security data by offering a comprehensive picture of your system's cyberhealth and protection status. The dashboard also shows the distribution of security events by various criteria, effectively highlighting the most affected systems, patterns, and users to help you focus resources where they are needed most.

To ensure the information is tailored to your specific needs, you can also create your own personalized monitoring pages with a selection of charts that are most relevant to your responsibilities..

Picture 4. Management-focused monitoring dashboard

For more information, see: Using the Monitoring Dashboard

SAP International Trade

Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For businesses operating with plants abroad, ensuring the correct export declaration is crucial. In a plant abroad scenario, this feature ensures that the country of the delivering plant, rather than the country of the company code, determines the correct foreign trade organization for the customs declaration. This enhancement improves compliance with international trade regulations for businesses leveraging the "Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad" (RITA) scope.

Value Proposition
  • Enhance compliance with trade regulations by ensuring the correct foreign trade organization is used for export declarations.
Capabilities 
  • Manage your export declaration in SAP Global Trade Services using the foreign trade organization within the country of the plant abroad.
  • Identify plants abroad on pro forma invoice line items by considering countries where registration for indirect taxation (RITA) is enabled.

In SAP Cloud ERP,  a billing document exists where goods delivery is performed via a foreign plant, for example, the plant country is different from the country of the company code.

Picture 5. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

In SAP GTS, Edition for SAP HANA, the FTO of the corresponding export declaration is now determined based on the legal unit corresponding to the foreign plant used in the billing document.

Picture 6. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For more information, see: Enablement of Plants-Abroad for Export Processing and Foreign Plant​

Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

Continuous compliance with sanction party lists is critical. Screening requests sent to SAP Watch List Screening will now remain active for delta screening for up to one month. This means the system automatically performs delta screenings against any changes to watch lists and updates the status of your business partners and documents accordingly, providing automatic coverage without requiring new manual screenings.

Additionally, for situations requiring manual intervention, the "Manage Documents - Trade Compliance" Fiori app now allows authorized users to manually release a trade compliance document item that is blocked. This creates a simple, straightforward, and auditable overrule process to comply with legal requirements while ensuring business continuity.

Picture 7. Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

For more information, see: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening and Delta Screening in SAP Watch List Screening

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on Hiruni Arunodhi Badulle | LinkedIn.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM vs CHARM in managing one landscape with...
entry.content:
Hi Maurizio,

I didn' t understand properly what is your point about this scenario  - my understanding is that we already have it (the lock inside one system) covered with the standard solution.


From the section you mentioned :
"Regardless of whether you use a separate development system or share the same system for your projects, you must avoid different teams working on the same object concurrently if the changes are to be imported in the same production system. It can lead to inconsistencies. The object must stay locked until it has been imported in the production system."
I understand that it only emphasizes that there should not be two different teams (i.e. two "projects") changing the same object - it seems to me that you are considering that CSOL would not cover this in ALM.

My understanding is that this is assured inside each system alone by the structure of Transport Request (TR) itself:  When you create a TR, you will have one or more Tasks below it. Once you change an object, this will be set as blocked in the level of the Task, until you release it. At this moment the block will be moved to the upper-level (TR), so you have under that TR (or their Tasks) the block control for the object.
If someone tries to change that object and assign it to another TR, the standard system (not sure if the name is correct, but I would call it Transport System) will prevent that you assign that object to another TR, thus assuring what you are aiming for - prevent 2 different changes touching the same object.

You can have 2 users changing the same object, provided their Tasks are under the same TR (= same project), once they release their Taks, the block control moves up to the TR.
There are ways to bypass this block control, this can be handled by authorizations, etc.. , this is another discussion.

So, as far as I could understand your requirement, the standard system provides the solution inside one system. If this is not what you need, kindly enlighten.

What CSOL provides is a separate (external) control to manage different systems and hold a "copy" of the objects blocked in each managed system - if there is already one (system) blocking an object and you try to change it in another one, it will provide ways to prevent that (= cross-system lock is active). For me, it only makes sense to use this in a multi-system scenario, as in case of only one system, the check (my guess) would be performed before the check from CSOL.

Does it make sense?

 

P.S.: I am looking for alternatives to ChaRM, once it is (supposed to) end in 2027. If you know a replacement from SAP, I'd appreciate if you can share the knowledge 🙂

 

Kind regards,

Eduardo Hartmann

entry.title: Re: can we rerun SU25 step 2a,2b,2c
entry.content:
Refer to the guidance shown in the below KBA

https://me.sap.com/notes/3431747

SAP's recommendations to perform the SU25 upgrade post processing:

  1. The SU25 upgrade post processing must be executed in the Development system.
    Afterwards the changes must be transported along the system landscape.

  2. Every SU25 report has a detailed documentation on the initial SU25 screen and in each step.
    SAP highly recommends to read the documentations for every step before it is started.
    The INFO-button on the top of the screen of each step leads to the documentation pop-up.

  3. The recommended order for the SU25 upgrade post processing:
    1. Before the start, a backup of the current situation shall be created by using Step 3. Such a backup should always be created and an additional one after reaching each mile stone of the processing of SU25.
    2. Mandatory for new installations:
      Step 1:       Initial fill of customer tables: SAP delivers updated USOBX and USOBT table content. In a new installation, this step is used to fill the customer tables USOBX_C and USOBT_C initially.
      This step shall by used only for new installations or to reset customer data to the SAP default values.
      Caution: Executing this step in upgraded systems, will delete also custom made changes! 
      For existing/upgraded systems:
      Step 2a: Automatic Comparison with SU22 data: Always mandatory in upgraded systems.
    3. Step 2b: Modification Adjustments with SU22 data: This step is always mandatory in upgraded systems.
      (-->now its the time to create another backup transport with step 3 of SU25 and to export it.)
    4. Before Step 2c, the report SU24_AUTO_REPAIR shall be used. To avoid data loss, the corrections of SAP note 3526980 must be present in the system before running the report. The report and its functions are documented in SAP Note 1539556 [A]. The selection screen of the report shall be filled according to the requirements of the system. In section 'See also' of this KBA there is a screenshot of a possible variant.
      (-->backup transport with step 3 of SU25 and export it.)
    5. Step 2c is highly recommended. 
      In this step the list of affected roles is provided.
      Remark: Because changing the authorization concept can take time, the role SAP_NEW can be generated and used as interim workaround for the users affected by the changes.
      This enables your business processes to continue uninterrupted until the changes are finished. Once the upgrade post processing is finished, that role SAP_NEW shall be removed from users again. 
    6. Step 2d: There is no recommendation for this step because it's optional.
    7. Step 3: Export the final status and transport the changes along the system landscape (DEV->QAS, PRD,..)
    8. Transport the updated roles to the test system, and after successfully tested to the production system.

Tip regarding step 2c and 2d:
In the SU25 documentation the following information can be read:
Making menu changes in 2d can lead to applications whose SU24 data was
updated in steps 2a and 2b or using Expert mode. It therefore makes
sense to execute 2d before 2c in order to obtain the maximum possible
worklist in step 2c.

Tip for systems where SU24 is not in use:
The recommendations from above are valid for systems where SU24 data is used.
If SU24 data is not used, then the SU25 upgrade post process is not mandatory.

entry.title: Re: Production Order Confirmation Posts Successfully but...
entry.content:
Dear @Shivanshu,

What you’re seeing is standard behawior in Public Cloud it’s not a API/EML (RAP BO) limitation. In Public Cloud goods movements created from a production order confirmation including 261 backflush/GI are processed in a decoupled background step (update/background processing) separate from the confirmation save. SAP explicitly says this background processing applies not only to the Fiori confirmation app but also to BAPI/API/mass processing/COGI so your EML call can succeed and still not immediately post the 261 and you won’t necessarily get a synchronous backend error back in the EML response

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003602391

Where to look:

SAP Note for Public Cloud says to check exactly this chain:

1. CO14 Display Production Order Confirmation select the confirmation Goods Movements

2. If movements show as blue lines they are in pre/postprocessing

3. Then check CO1P (Predefined Confirmation Processes) and/or COGI (Reprocess Goods Movements) and post/reprocess from there

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003519481

If you still don’t see errors immediately:

  • Background job needed/helps. SAP recommends using Schedule Posting of Predefined Confirmation Processes to process goods movements after confirmations/cancellations more reliably and to clean up lock-related issues

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003336586

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003402211

Best Regards,

Dawid

Marcel

entry.title: Preparing for SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release – Universal Full Profile View
entry.content:
With the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release, the Latest People Profile – Full Profile View will become universally available for all customers. As part of this change, the enablement status will shift from Customer Configured to Automatically On, meaning Full Profile View will be enabled by default and no longer controlled by a system setting.

While the Full Profile View will be turned on automatically, customers must still complete required configuration activities to ensure the experience is aligned with their business needs.

Action is required prior to the 1H 2026 universal push. Join this session to understand what preparation is required, what typically comes up as asks and use the chance to ask questions.

Customer Registration required: Microsoft Virtual Events Powered by Teams

entry.title: Re: CAP-consuming on-premise OData API : read ECONNRESET...
entry.content:
First of all: the 8443 port is only the port, where the SCC listens for browser connections for its administrative UI, it is not used anywhere for productive OData calls...

"Connection Reset" is a standard TCP/IP error message that means a connection got broken either by network problems (firewall, etc.) or because the communication partner crashed.

But here we have basically three connections:
CAP --> Connectivity Agent
Connectivity Agent <--> SCC (internet tunnel)
SCC --> backend system

and without further traces, it will be hard to make progress here. Check the SCC logs whether you can see the incoming connection here and any possible network problems with the backend system. If nothing can be seen here, the problem must be in one of the earlier steps. Then request the logs of the Connectivity Agent and check them.

entry.title: Re: Feedback on SAP S/4HANA PP & Manufacturing Certificat...
entry.content:
Hi Mohamed,

I think there are still very few who have taken this very assessment that has only been available for a short time.
I have completed a certification under the new format but one that was not based on a practical system. (Data Analyst SAC)

To your questions:

should we rely mainly on SAP Learning Journeys/e-learning courses?
It is feasible to learn this purely with Learning Journeys, it all depends on your experience and which certification.

Is hands-on system experience mandatory to pass the exam?
In this case definitely YES

Are there any recommended preparation strategies or resources?
It is in each case individual, either it is enough to refresh your knowledge with Learning Journeys or it is better to complete a presence or online presence training. Then there are also the Learning group forums which can help you. In short, there are several individual preparation options, you have to decide for yourself which one is best for you.

Finally, I understand that the multiple choice exam (QCM) is still available?
No, to my knowledge there is only scenario based (AI roleplay or Recorded Video) or system-based assessment. In your case only system-based.

If my Answers helped you, thanks for accepting the solution and a like. 

Have a nice day

Jan

entry.title: The Future of Enterprise Engagement is Here - SAP Engagement Cloud Q1 Product Release is Live!
entry.content:
Today, we’re proud to introduce the SAP Engagement Cloud Q1 2026 Release. You’ll see bold innovation across channels, intelligence, analytics, and data operations. This release accelerates our mission to help brands Power Unique Engagement by connecting data, AI, and orchestration at enterprise scale.

SAP Engagement Cloud represents the evolution of SAP Emarsys into a more deeply aligned, enterprise-ready solution built for SAP customers navigating complex, global engagement needs. Customers will continue to rely on the powerful capabilities they know and trust—now with even more AI-powered innovations.  

“Consumers expect brands to respond and interact with them in real time. Thanks to SAP Engagement Cloud and SAP Commerce Cloud, we never have to worry about performance issues during high-traffic peaks and can be there for our customers.” 

Naresh Krishnamurthy 
Senior Manager, Business Transformation, Cosmetics, Molton Brown 

With our Q1 release, we’re introducing even more capabilities that are practical, scalable, and built for enterprise teams. Engagement is no longer a campaign exercise; it’s a competitive differentiator. Customers move more fluidly between channels, devices, and moments, and brands must orchestrate experiences that feel connected in real time.  

SAP Engagement Cloud provides the intelligence, orchestration, and scale that organizations need to deliver personalized engagement across every interaction. This year, SAP has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Personalization Engines for the 7th consecutive time. We believe this reaffirms our commitment to AI-driven personalization, customer trust, and measurable outcomes.

Connected: Omnichannel Execution That Scales 

Our second conversational channel, LINE, is now generally available through our partner Sinch. You can send text and card messages, trigger real-time interactions, and expand into the high-growth Asia-Pacific region with confidence. This means you can meet customers where they already are, including fast-moving conversational channels that drive higher responses and deeper engagement.  

Real conversations create real results. That’s why we’ve made Conversational Messaging for both WhatsApp and LINE generally available, enabling real-time, interactive conversations with your contacts. For example, you can capture preferences, branch journeys based on responses, and follow up instantly with personalized messages, turning conversations into meaningful interactions. 

For new customers, our Brand Center now supports Web Channel and Mobile In-app experiences with unified themes, content blocks, and visual identity elements. Using these features, your brand can stay consistent while your team saves hours of repetitive design and QA work. In the longer term, potential additions include:  

  • Support for Mobile In‑app multi‑step campaigns 
  • Form confirmation pages in Web Channel 
  • Expanded channel coverage 

While new customers can start using these capabilities now, existing customers won’t be far behind—we’ll support a full transition and asset migration next. Existing customers can also join our Early Adopter Program today. Connect with your Customer Success Manager to learn more.  
 

“We need partners like SAP Engagement Cloud who help us understand this entire journey throughout the year so that on special days like Black Friday, we can reach the right customer at the right time and through the most appropriate channel.” 

Nadiele Serpa da Silva, CRM Coordinator, Arezzo 

SAP Engagement Cloud SDK 

We’re piloting the SAP Engagement Cloud SDK, an industry-first toolkit that unifies integration for Android, iOS, and Web into a single, natively cross-platform solution. This toolkit streamlines integration, improves performance, and brings first-party, uniform data collection across all mobile and web touchpoints. This starts with Mobile Push, Web Push, and In-app messaging.  

  • We’re seeking pilot participants with native Android, iOS, or Kotlin Multiplatform apps to test with at least two channels. Contact your Customer Success Manager to learn more.  

For teams using the Expo framework for React Native, we’ve launched the Emarsys SDK Expo Plugin pilot to deliver complete Mobile Engage capabilities without complex workarounds. This means faster time-to-value and a smoother integration experience.  

  • Customers using Mobile Engage and the Expo framework are welcome to join the pilot. Contact your Customer Success Manager to learn more. 

Insightful: AI & Analytics That Deliver Clarity 

We’re releasing the predictive Mobile Push engagement template in AI Segmentation. This allows you to target contacts who are likely to engage, become, or remain inactive via the Mobile Push channel. Use this to focus your efforts on what will drive the strongest results. 

We’ve also enhanced the AI‑assisted Report Builder to support the SMS channel. It uses a new concept of data context to narrow the scope to a dataset, like SMS campaigns, so answers are more relevant and accurate. This helps turn reporting into a conversation instead of a chore. You can ask questions such as, “What was SMS revenue last month?” and get an answer in seconds.  

In addition, we’ve added visual logic previews to the AI‑assisted Report Builder, so you can clearly see how your questions are interpreted. This helps ensure accuracy, transparency, and confidence in every report you generate. For example, you can see the conditions and confirm the logic of this question: “What are sent, delivered, clicked, unsubscribed, and revenue for last‑month SMS campaigns with a click rate above 5 percent and campaign name starting with “UK,” ordered by revenue?” 

On the analytics side, Conversational Channel LINE is fully integrated into Revenue Attribution. This gives you even more visibility into real drivers of conversions, such as send and click data. You can quickly pull reports for your executives to see the revenue impact and how conversational channels contribute to business outcomes.  

Event Attribution now includes WhatsApp and LINE, which gives you a complete view of how interactions across channels and activities influence conversions. For example, you can attribute a premium plan with subscription across SMS, in‑app, LINE, and WhatsApp to see which campaigns and messages drive the most conversions.  

Adaptive: A Composable Platform Built for Flexibility 

The new Engagement Events data view in Open Data exposes all ingested events. This enhancement makes it easy to connect engagement data to your analytics stack for richer reporting and insights. In addition, you can get deeper analysis and reporting of event details, such as IDs, timestamps, contact references, and JSON payloads. 

We’ve also advanced the Standard Product Catalog API pilot with new features. We have introduced a capability to remove incorrect product records in the Standard Product Catalog API pilot via a public deletion endpoint. Deletions are handled safely, and the API Monitoring tool shows the recent count of removed items. 

In addition, Standard Product Data is available in Open Data for pilot customers. This provides query able access to product identities, attributes, pricing, availability, localization, and a full audit trail for deeper reporting and troubleshooting. You can track price drops over time or retrieve all details for a specific item. 

  • Explore the Standard Product Catalog API by joining our pilot program. 

These enhancements matter because better data leads to better recommendations. Combined with improved reporting, you’ll have fewer operational headaches, too.  

Trusted: Enterprise-Grade Control & Governance 

The Asset Tagging tool is now available in Segmentation. This helps you organize and surface audiences faster using searchable tags like brand, region, or product type. As a result, your teams stay better aligned, organized, and efficient—even as your audience strategies grow more complex. 
 

“We see each other as equal partners and are highly motivated to keep growing together. […] We receive a great deal of support on technical projects, which increases our speed. And I don't think you can do that with just any tool. This is a great advantage for us, and it makes our partnership with SAP Engagement Cloud truly special.” 

Julia Piep, Head of CRM, flaconi 

In addition, Business Areas let users control who can access customer data, content, and campaigns by brand or region—all within a single account through role‑based authorizations. 

What’s Next for SAP Engagement Cloud? 

This release marks the start of a transformational year. Follow along as we continue to invest in:  

  • Real-time decisioning scaled across the entire SAP ecosystem 
  • Closed-loop learning systems that refine personalization with every interaction 
  • Expanded APIs and deeper interoperability across SAP Customer Experience (CX) 
  • Even more simplicity for marketers and extensibility for developers 

The future of engagement is connected, intelligent, and enterprise-wide. And SAP Engagement Cloud is built to lead that future.

entry.title: Core Logistics in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP where we have introduced a set of enhancements focused on strengthening day-to-day supply chain execution and planning. These updates help streamline key logistics activities and improve user experience, so teams can execute faster, improve material visibility, and make more reliable, data-driven decisions across the end-to-end logistics flow.  In this blog post, you'll find an overview of the key new innovations and highlight features being rolled out with this release.

Short on time? The below video gives a summary of the top 3 highlights from this release.


 

 



Inventory Management

In the inventory management area, new features and apps have bene introduced to improve efficiency and accuracy when managing and moving physical inventory.

Streamlining Physical Inventory Management with new app "Manage Physical Inventory Item List" 

Managing physical inventory is about far more than counting stock: it’s about ensuring every item is tracked, verified, and processed correctly across potentially hundreds or thousands of line items, multiple locations, and multiple documents. In this kind of environment, speed and accuracy are critical. With this in mind, in the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP, SAP introduces the new Fiori app Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634)—giving inventory managers one clear, powerful place to manage physical inventory items across all relevant documents. The new app is designed to bring clarity and structure to this time-sensitive process by offering intuitive, user-friendly list reports and comprehensive status visibility so users can immediately see what needs attention and what’s already complete.

When you open the app, you start with flexible filtering options that let you tailor the list to the exact scenario you’re working on—such as narrowing down by plant and storage location to generate an overview for a specific area. Filters can be expanded easily, making it simple to adapt the screen to different use cases. From the resulting list, you can select items for further processing and execute key follow-up actions directly in the same place—including posting, triggering a recount, or printing—without jumping between applications. This is where the app really shines: it enables managers to filter physical inventory items by processing status (for example, showing only Counted items) so they can focus on the next required step and move work forward efficiently across multiple inventory documents.

A typical end-to-end scenario highlights the productivity gains. After physical inventory documents have been created and warehouse staff have completed counting, the inventory manager can filter by planned count date and then refine the list by status—such as showing only counted items. From there, it’s easy to review count details, identify quantity differences, and decide the right follow-up action. Items with no difference can be mass-selected and posted in just a click. Items with large discrepancies can be selected and sent for recount, where the user defines a recount date and (if needed) adds header information—automatically generating a new physical inventory document so warehouse teams know exactly what to recount. For marginal differences, the manager can still proceed with posting based on business rules, and if the system is configured with reason codes, the app prompts users to select the appropriate code during posting—supporting compliant, auditable processing aligned with company requirements. Finally, managers can confirm outcomes by filtering for posted items and, when needed, navigate directly from the list to the created material documents for review and further processing.

Overall, Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634) increases inventory transparency and helps teams complete inventory processing faster and with fewer errors. By combining comprehensive list reporting, efficient cross-document processing, and status-driven filtering in a single, streamlined experience, the app supports inventory managers with the clarity they need to keep physical inventory moving from count to resolution—accurately, quickly, and with full control.

 

 

Supporting Intra-Plant Stock Transfer Reservations

In many plants, materials have to be moved between storage locations to keep production and operations running—often at short notice and under time pressure. While these movements are typically planned using reservations (created manually or generated by MRP), execution has historically required warehouse staff to switch between apps or rely on classic processing such as MIGO—adding effort and increasing the risk of mistakes. With this new enhancement, intra-plant stock transfers become significantly easier and faster: warehouse teams can now use the Manage Reservation Items app to post stock transfers directly from a reservation for both manual and MRP-generated reservations using movement type 311, all in one place.

In practice, users filter and review the relevant reservation items, select multiple lines at once, and create the goods movement as a single, streamlined action—completing any missing details as needed before posting. Once posted, the system automatically creates two material document entries—one for the goods issue and one for the goods receipt—so the transfer is fully captured and traceable without extra steps. The result is clear business value: mass posting of several reservation items in one go, improved usability through integrated goods movement posting, and more time-efficient processing for busy warehouse teams.

 

Monitor Material Documents More Effectively : Material Documents Overview Enhancements 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP’s Material Documents Overview app (F1077) is a modern SAP Fiori replacement for the classic MB51 experience—built to help you review and analyze goods movements with a smoother, more intuitive UX while closing remaining functional gaps step by step. In one place, users can search, filter, sort, and drill into material documents to understand what moved, when it posted, and how it impacts operations and stock. This transition is more than “nice to have”: moving to the new app is essential because legacy options will ultimately be deprecated, and SAP is actively enhancing F1077 based on real customer feedback to make switching worthwhile.

Driven directly by customer input, SAP has been evolving the app’s functional scope and usability to better match how supply chain and warehouse teams actually work. Recent enhancements include the availability of additional fields—notably amount in local currency and sales value—to make financial and commercial context visible alongside logistics data. The app also now supports signed quantity, reducing the mental gymnastics of interpreting directionality across receipts and issues. And for users who regularly pivot between quantity-focused and value-focused analysis, SAP introduced a variant that displays amounts and values, making it easier to switch perspectives and tailor the overview to different roles and reporting needs.

In the 2602 release, the app takes a more visual and analytical leap forward with two planned improvements that make high-volume review work noticeably smoother. First, color coding for selected quantities (such as Signed Quantity)helps users identify the right figures at a glance, reducing errors and speeding up interpretation during daily checks. Second, sums for quantities and values add instant rollups for quick validation and comparison—particularly useful when reconciling multiple postings or checking totals across filtered selections. Together, these 2602 features continue the push to improve usability while enhancing the functional scope in the goods movement process, keeping the Material Documents Overview app firmly positioned as a practical tool for operational control in SAP Cloud ERP.

 


Handling Unit Management

Effective Handling Unit Management is fundamental to an integrated and transparent supply chain. Our latest updates to SAP Cloud ERP introduce significant enhancements in this area, designed to increase operational flexibility, improve system integration, and ensure alignment with global standards

Terminology Alignment with Global Standards for SSCC

To promote clarity and ensure consistency with global industry practices, we have updated the terminology used in the configuration activities for Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) numbering. The term "ILN basis number" has been replaced with "GS1 company prefix." This alignment with current GS1 standards helps reduce ambiguity during system setup and ensures your organizations handling unit identification is compliant with international norms. This change enhances usability for global enterprises without altering any underlying system logic.

Interface Support for Empty Handling Unit Creation

To provide greater flexibility for integration scenarios, the OData API for Handling Unit Management now supports the creation of empty handling units. This enhancement enables external systems or custom applications to first create a handling unit container and then subsequently manage the packing of materials into it. This capability allows organizations to model real-world logistics processes more accurately, where containers are often prepared before their contents are finalized. This facilitates more sophisticated and responsive integration with warehouse automation systems and third-party logistics platforms.

 

New Business Event for Handling Unit Creation

We are introducing a new business event that is triggered upon the creation of a handling unit within an Inventory Management-managed storage location. This event-driven mechanism enables real-time communication with external systems, such as parcel service providers or transportation management platforms, instantly notifying them when a handling unit is ready. This capability allows organizations to automate downstream processes, improve the timeliness of data exchange, and enhance end-to-end visibility, thereby reducing manual coordination and improving overall supply chain responsiveness.

 

Integration with External Output Management Systems

This release introduces a new feature that allows for the integration of Handling Unit Management with external, best-of-breed output management systems. By enabling the external channel in your output parameter determination, you can now send raw data for handling unit labels and documents directly to a preferred third-party solution. This provides organizations with maximum flexibility to design complex form layouts, manage specialized printing hardware, and utilize advanced output channels. The external system processes the output and sends a status confirmation back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ensuring full process traceability and control.

 

 

Further Services to Support Partial Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery with Third-Party Logistics Warehouse Management (Release 2508.2) 

We are excited to introduce a significant enhancement to our warehouse logistics capabilities: the ability to post partial goods receipts for inbound deliveries from your third-party Warehouse Management System (WMS). This feature directly addresses a common bottleneck at the receiving dock by empowering your warehouse staff to begin the putaway process immediately after unloading parts of a delivery, rather than waiting to check the entire shipment. This new flexibility accommodates a wide range of scenarios, allowing you to process complete or fractional quantities for single or multiple delivery items, both with and without handling units. This ultimately accelerates inventory availability and dramatically improves warehouse throughput.

To ensure seamless data integrity, this process is supported by robust, automated communication between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and your WMS. For every partial receipt posted, a confirmation is sent to the WMS, and a straightforward process allows for the cancellation of any erroneous postings. Once all parts of the delivery have been received, the inbound delivery in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is automatically updated to reflect the final, accurate totals for quantity, weight, and volume. A final confirmation is then sent to the WMS, guaranteeing that your core ERP system is perfectly synchronized with the physical reality documented by your warehouse partner.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find all our assets

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: process approachfor: Empties Staging with EWM
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

in a process we need to deal with certain customer racks as empties/returnables. in these racks there is e.g. 102 lb. from production side we want to stage only full racks. what is the smartes way to handle this?

currently: we pack 1 pc of packmaterial inside a hu and trigger a staging with event-driven kanban and the help of a 1 pc product wt in order to bring a whole rack. this comes unfortunately with several downsides. e.g. you can't use bulk storage since here you need to deal with hu wt's or you can't use standard customizing to prevent mixed sotrage because the racks are always mixed since the packing material is part of the hu in order to not lose it.

 

any thought's?

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: PMW with 4110 UTF-8 code page for arabic symbols disp...
entry.content:
Good day @Natalia_Sverchinskaya 

Thank you for your question.

When Arabic characters show as dots at the bank, it is almost always one of these two causes:

  • The file is not truly delivered as UTC 8 end to end or
  • The bank’s pain.001 implementation does not support Arabic characters in the Name fields, even if the file is UTF8.

You can pinpoint what you might have missed by:

Confirming the XML is really UTF 8 and not only configured as 4110. For that generate the payment file in the Manage Payment Media, then open the XML in Notepad++ or any editor that has similar function. Check the first line contains something like: encoding= “UTF-8”. If you see UTF 8 in the header but the editor detects a different encoding, you have an encoding conversion in the download or transfer chain.

See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1730272 for more details.

Second, verify that transfer channel is not converting your file. This could be how your email handles attachment, how you copy paste into blank portal, your middleware converting to ISO-8859- or Windows- encodings, your file being saved by your text editor as “ANSI”. Do not change from UTF 8 if you use BTP integration Suite or SFTP. See https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/avoiding-encoding-issues for more details.

Some banks accept technical accepts Arabic in the file but render unsupported characters as dots or reject them during validation. You can carry out a practical test by:

  • Putting an Arabic string into Remittance information and keep Dbtr/Nm in Latin only.
  • If remittance displays correctly but debtor names becomes dots, the bank’s restriction is one name fields, not on UTF 8 itself.

Finally, in some CGI XML formats, the following reference provides details on handling of special characters, which can impact what ends up in the output. You may need to align with your banks guidance if they expect specific handling rather than only changing code page.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1988783

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Reversal of partial settlement incorrect on account assignment for accrual reversal condition
entry.content:
Dears

I am facing a problem that the system does not reverse a partial settlement (or final for that matter) because of unbalanced document.

For a French Sales Org the original document is as Z* condition types copied from standard

Header: 

Item with accruals reversal:

Account assignment for this item

00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
00000001 Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 50
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
00000001 Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 40
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 31
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
00000001 Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCS1)

The reversal document looks like:

Header:

Same item as before:

 

Account assignment for the same item including main messages with the error

FI/CO Interface: Balance in Transaction Currency 28.101,00- (EUR)
Document INT0000001 has status C and is complete
Chart of accounts SGL determined for company code FR01
The posting document is created with transaction RMRP (Incoming invoice)
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 40
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 50
_________________________________________________________________________
Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 21
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCS1)

As you can see in the reversal - the red marked line is trying to post to the same G/L account with posting Key 50 (besides the 40) and because of this the document is unbalanced.

The settings for the condition contract are shared amongst several sales orgs and if I do the same for a German Sales Org (again same settings - same G/L accounts) the system reverts the document without any issue.

This occurs only in reversal of partial settlement and not in any other cases.

Any idea on how we can see why the system is trying to post with that other account key 50 besides de correct one of 40 (that reverts the original document posting)?

entry.title: How to use Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Searching through countless SAP Notes and Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) can feel overwhelming, especially when you’re looking for something specific. The good news? SAP for Me’s Expert Search feature makes this task much simpler. 

 

Before diving in, let’s clarify what SAP Notes and SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs) are. 

  • SAP Notes describe a software issue and its solution. They include a description of the symptoms, the cause of the error, and the SAP release and support package level in which the error occurs. Depending on the type of error, an SAP Note may also include workarounds and links to support packages that solve the problem, and most SAP Notes contain coding corrections. 
  • SAP Knowledge Base Articles are practical "how-to" guides. Using business language, KBAs answer frequently asked questions arising from your daily work with SAP software. No coding corrections or modifications are provided. 

 

 Why Use Expert Search? 

The “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” in SAP for Me provides an at-a-glance view on relevant updates, helps you organize your favorite notes, and allows you to retrieve notes based on advanced selection criteria, especially as a recurrent task.  This feature is extremely helpful if you carry out regular analyses of relevant notes.

 

Getting Started in 7 Simple Steps:  

  1. Login to SAP for Me with your S-user ID and password. 
  2. From the left-hand dashboard, navigate to Services & Support. 
  3. Click on the KBAs & Notes tab. 
  4. Select the “SAP Notes & KBAs Expert Search” card. 
  5. Click on the “Expert Search” tab.  

     

  6. Use the filter bar to specify your selection criteria. 
    • Example: Components (Starts): BC-DB*.  
    • As you begin typing the component key, the auto-completion feature will assist you in specifying the correct component. Only notes assigned to components that start with the entered key will be displayed. 
  7. Finally, click the “Go” button to run your search.  

 

Note: No special authorization is required in your S-user ID to use the Expert Search function in SAP for Me.  You can apply different filter criteria, such as Search Term, Software Component (Soft Comp), Component (Exact), and more. 

 

For more information, please refer to the Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2388433- Information about using Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs - SAP for Me. 

 

Additional Resources:  

  • Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 2478289 - How to set up notifications for new or updated SAP Notes and/or KBAs with Expert Search filters - SAP for Me 

 

Got More Questions? 

 Please note that the Customer Interaction Center are always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!    
 

If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community  

 

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts  

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming CIC Webcasts. These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, etc.  

Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.  

 

Lastly, you may want to access all CIC Blogs here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/CustomerInteractionCenter/tg-p/board-id/crm-blog-sap.  

Thank you for reading!  We hope you find this guide helpful.

entry.title: Re: Assignment Profile - Assign only to new job classific...
entry.content:
Hi Louis, could one option be to set up an assignment profile with the below rules:

  • Job Classification = [the job classification you're interested in]
  • User ID =/= [list of employees who currently have this job classification]

That way, it only picks up people who move into that Job Classification from now on, excluding the list of people who already have it. Although I always try to avoid creating APs with User IDs, in this scenario it sounds like it would be fine because it's just a static one-time list, rather than trying to capture a dynamic group of employees. 

If you were then to Download Preview Users, the expected result is that no users would be captured by this AP initially, until a 'new' person gets allocated to that Job Classification.

EDIT: I've just reread your scenario and on a second read it sounds like you're not talking about one specific Job Classification but instead about people moving from any Job Classification into another - in which case please ignore my answer!

entry.title: SAP TM Report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG adaptation After System Copy or Refresh
entry.content:
SAP TM Report BSG adaptation After System Copy or Refresh

Introduction

Problem Overview

After performing a system copy or refresh in SAP environments, administrators frequently encounter critical issues where the "LOGQS" field in table "/SAPAPO/LOCMAP" continues to point to the source system instead of the target system. For example, the field may still reference "XXPXXXX100" (production) when it should point to "XXQXXXX100" (quality/test system). This misalignment disrupts location mapping functionality and can cause significant integration issues between ECC/S/4HANA.

The symptoms typically manifest as business partner errors, failed CIF integrations, and location master data inconsistencies that prevent proper supply chain planning operations. Users may experience "BP does not exist" errors when testing in a refreshed environment, even though the data exists but with incorrect logical system references.

Technical Background

The LOGQS field in /SAPAPO/LOCMAP represents the Business System Group (BSG) assignment for location master data in SAP Transportation Management systems. This field maintains the logical connection between source systems (ECC/S/4HANA), ensuring accurate data flow for supply chain operations.

During system copy or refresh operations, the location mapping table retains the original production logical system references, creating a mismatch between the copied data and the target system's expected logical system configuration. This occurs because standard system copies procedures focus on data replication but don't automatically adjust system-specific configuration fields like LOGQS.

Root Cause Analysis

The issue stems from the standard behavior of system copy operations, which replicate table contents without adjusting environment-specific logical system references. The /SAPAPO/LOCMAP table maintains BSG assignments that are environment-dependent, but these references aren't automatically updated during the copy process.

When production data is copied to quality or test systems, the BSG details reflect the production configuration while the target environment expects different BSG assignments. This creates a fundamental disconnect in the location mapping framework.

Step-by-Step Resolution Process

Pre-Resolution Checks

  1. Verify Current LOGQS Values: Access table /SAPAPO/LOCMAP via SE16N to identify entries with incorrect logical system references.

  1. Identify Target System Configuration: Determine the correct BSG and logical system values for the target environment
  2. Create System Backup: Ensure proper backup procedures are in place before making configuration changes

Correction Steps

  1. Access Resolution Tool: Execute transaction SE38 and run report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG
  2. Configure Parameters: Set the old logical system (source) and new logical system (target) parameters in the report
  3. Execute Update Process: Run the report to systematically update LOGQS field values in /SAPAPO/LOCMAP
  4. Monitor Progress: Review the report output to ensure all relevant entries are processed correctly

Post-Resolution Validation

  • Integration Testing: Verify CIF connectivity between ECC/S/4HANAsystems functions properly
  • Location Master Verification: Confirm location master data displays correctly in transactions like /SAPAPO/LOC3
  • Business Process Testing: Execute end-to-end supply chain planning scenarios to validate complete functionality

Alternative Solutions

For environments requiring more comprehensive logical system changes, consider using BDLS (Logical System Name Conversion) which addresses logical system references across multiple tables, However, this approach requires careful consideration of all affected applications and thorough testing.

The report /SAPAPO/CHANGE_LOGSYS provides specific functionality for location master logical system changes, though it requires careful review of the source code comments to understand the full impact. Additional reports may be needed for related mapping tables like /SAPAPO/MATMAP, /SAPAPO/PPMMAP, and /SAPAPO/RESKEY.

Impact Assessment

Business Impact

Incorrect LOGQS field values can severely disrupt supply chain planning operations, causing failures in demand planning, supply network planning, and transportation management processes. Business users may experience system errors when accessing location-dependent functionality, leading to planning delays and operational inefficiencies.

Technical Implications

The misalignment affects DRF integration stability, potentially causing queue processing errors and data synchronization failures between integrated systems. System performance may degrade due to failed integration attempts and error handling overhead.

Prevention Strategies

System Copy Best Practices

  • Comprehensive Planning: Include logical system mapping updates in standard system copy procedures,
  • Documentation Requirements: Maintain detailed records of logical system assignments for each environment
  • Validation Checklists: Implement post-copy validation procedures that specifically verify LOGQS field accuracy

Monitoring and Maintenance

  • Regular Health Checks: Schedule periodic reviews of location mapping table consistency
  • Automated Monitoring: Implement monitoring solutions that detect logical system misalignments
  • Change Management: Establish procedures for managing logical system changes across system landscapes

Conclusion

SAP Note 3595300 addresses a critical post-system-copy issue that can significantly impact supply chain operations. The resolution involves using the dedicated report /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_BSG to correct LOGQS field values in the location mapping table. Success requires careful planning, systematic execution, and thorough validation to ensure complete integration stability.

System administrators should incorporate these correction procedures into standard system copy workflows and implement monitoring mechanisms to prevent similar issues in future refresh operations. Proper attention to logical system consistency ensures reliable supply chain planning functionality across all system environments.

Refer:

  • SAP Note 3315995: Location: Report BSG adaptation
  • SAP Note 3396965: Changing Logical System (LOGSYS) for table /SAPAPO/LOCMAP
  • SAP Note 1909192: How to change variants of CIF integration model after system copy

Hope that can help you.

Thanks, and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with Microsoft Po...
entry.content:
Hello @Mike_vanIngen 

Thank you for your question.

Signing in to Power BI’s OData Feed and organisational account is not a generic OAuth 2.0 client. It expects Azure AD style OAuth approach and specifically requires the service to return a WWW-Authenticate header that contains an authorisation URL posting to login-microsoftonline.com. If that header is missing or different, Power BI throws exactly that “The WWW-Authenticate header doesn't contain a valid authorization URL.” you see.

In Public Cloud, OAuth for inbound API access is designed for system to system OAuth patterns (comm arrangement OAuth details, token endpoint, scopes and in many cases OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertation or confidential client patterns), not for Power BI’s Azure AD specific organisational login flow. That explains why your basic Auth works, OAuth via Power BI OData fails. See https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-business-partner-oauthsamlbearer..html for more details.

Question: Are there specific prerequisites or configuration steps required?

Answer: You can configure OAuth on the S4H PC but Power BI OData Feed does not consume it unless the service behaves like Azure AD in the WWW-Authenticate challenge.

Question: Is SAP IAS officially supported for OAuth authentication with Power BI?

Answer: Not in the way Power BI’s organisational account expects. IAS is fine for SSO to SAP UIs but Power BI’s OData expects Azure AD endpoints and headers, not IAS

Question: Is this a known limitation of Power BI’s OAuth implementation?

Answer: Yes, Microsoft documents the exact WWW-Authenticate header pattern required for OData organisational sign in. If your service does not return this, Power BI won’t proceed. See Troubleshooting sign-in with OData feed for more details.

It is recommended to keep Basic Auth for OData. If your governance allows it, Basic Auth remains the most straightforward for Power BI OData Feed. You already confirmed it works.

Alternatively, you may consider using BTP as the integration layer, not direct Power BI OAuth. You can:

  1. Pull data from S4H Cloud APIs using BTP destinations.
  2. Land data in an analytics store, e.g., Datasphere, Azure SQL, etc.
  3. Connect Power BI to that store through Azure AD

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Is there a way to plan process orders in sequence by wk so that product A is made wk 1, prod b w2?
entry.content:
I work in a process industry where we have products that are "additive" for example, something like this:

Material A = Multi vitamin

Material B = Multi-vitamin + iron

Material C = Multi-vitamin + Iron and zinc

We would like to make these materials in sequence by week  For example:

Week 1 we make only Material A

week 2 we make Material B

week 3 we make material C

The reason is that if we make Material A, we can make material B without cleaning the equipment.  But if we make Material B, we cannot make Material A without cleaning the equipment because Material B has iron in it. It is the same with Material B and Material C.

We would like MRP to sequence these materials by week.  Weekly buckets don't work because it just buckets each material by week but doesn't separate them into different weeks.

Thank you so much for any replies.

entry.title: Re: Translation of User Defined Fields in B1
entry.content:
This is what I came up with. I'm a bit confused why the U_-prefix is not used thruout the system. Is there a table missing? It at least works as expected but the missing U_-part makes it look... hacky 😄

CREATE PROCEDURE "q_GetUserFieldTranslated"
/**
 * Return translatated user field based on Table and Language
 * Coded 2026-02-19 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	IN  TableName     NVARCHAR(30),   /* B1 Table ID */
	IN  UserField     NVARCHAR(30),   /* User fields name without U_-prefix! */
	IN  UserValue     NVARCHAR(100),  /* Value to translate */
	IN  LangInt       int             /* B1 system language ID */
)
	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
	SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	tSQL              NVARCHAR(1000);

BEGIN
	
	tSQL := 'SELECT T0."Descr", '''|| :LangInt ||''' AS "LangInt", T3."Trans"
		FROM          "UFD1" T0
			LEFT JOIN "CUFD" T1 ON (T0."FieldID" = T1."FieldID" AND T0."TableID" = T1."TableID")
			LEFT JOIN "OMLT" T2 ON T2."TableName" = ''UFD1''
			LEFT JOIN "MLT1" T3 ON (T2."TranEntry" = T3."TranEntry" AND T3."LangCode" = '''|| :LangInt ||''')
		WHERE T0."TableID"  = '''|| :TableName ||''' AND T0."FldValue" = '''|| :UserValue ||'''
	';
  
  EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL;
  
END;
