entry.title: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to create a new record in a custom MDF object (cust_mymdf) via the OData v2 API using a POST to the /upsert endpoint, with the goal of triggering the configured approval workflow.

What I'm trying to achieve:

  • Use the OData API to insert a termination request record as pending data.
  • Have the system's standard MDF workflow (configured on the object with a workflow group for approval) automatically trigger upon creation via API, so an approver receives a notification and can review/approve/reject.

Setup details:

  • Custom MDF object: cust_mymdf (effective-dated, externalCode as key field).
  • Pending Data = Yes on the object definition.
  • Workflow is configured directly on the object (have also tried business rule on save).
  • API user:   (non-admin/technical user, no "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission granted in RBP).
  • Authentication: Basic Auth (username (myuser@companyid) + password).

API call (via Postman):

  • Method: POST
  • URL:https://api55preview.sapsf.eu/odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true
  • Body (JSON):
{
  "__metadata": {
    "uri": "cust_mymdf(effectiveStartDate=datetime'2026-02-19T00:00:00',externalCode='19')",
    "type": "SFOData.cust_mymdf"
  },
  "externalCode": "19",
  "effectiveStartDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "externalName": "TestTerminate19Update",
  "cust_Employee": "B200394",
  "cust_TerminationDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "cust_OkToRehire": "Y",
  "cust_DeactivatePosition": "N"
}​

 

Result:

  • HTTP 200 OK
  • Response body:
{
  "d": [
    {
      "key": "cust_mymdf/effectiveStartDate=2026-02-19T00:00:00.000Z,cust_mymdf/externalCode=19",
      "status": "OK",
      "editStatus": "UPSERTED",
      "message": null,
      "index": 0,
      "httpCode": 200,
      "inlineResults": null
    }
  ]
}​
  • The record appears in Manage Data → Pending Records
  • No workflow is triggered — no approval request is created, no notification sent, nothing appears in Manage Workflow Requests or the approver's inbox.

    What I've already checked / tried:

    • The parameter ?workflowConfirmed=true is included in the URL (as required per sap documentation to simulate UI confirmation).
    • The API user does not have the "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission.
    • Manual creation via UI (Manage Data → Create new record for cust_MassTerminate) does trigger the workflow correctly (approver sees it).
    • Effective date is future-dated (2026-02-19), but same behavior occurs if I test with current date.
    • Workflow is set to trigger on create.
    • No duplicate pending records on the same key.

      According to SAP KBA 2396714 ("Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center") and community blogs (e.g., "Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API"), the combination of:

      • workflowConfirmed=trueand
      • API user without "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission

      should allow the workflow to trigger via API — but it's not happening in my case. Has anyone successfully triggered a custom MDF workflow via OData upsert in this scenario? Any additional prerequisites, payload tweaks, or known limitations I'm missing? Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated — thank you!

entry.title: Create a Sales Order with reference to Contract in cross Sales Area
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

I have a requirement wherein user will be creating a Global Contract, and the Sales Orders will be created with reference to the Contract but it will be Cross Sales Area.

Eg. I have created a Quantity Contract  for Amul India where Sales Area is 1000/10/10 and thereafter I will create a Sales Order for Amul Jamnagar with reference to the Contract wherein Amul Jamnagar belongs to 1001/10/10 and the 2nd Sales Order with reference to the Contract where the Customer is Amul Pune and so on.

We are on SAP RISE 2023.

I have tried using the Reference Sales Organisation /Reference Distribution Channel and Reference Division along with Common Distribn Channel and Common Division but nothing works.

I have tried Customer Hierarchy but that is also of no use eg.   1A Hierarchy is Amul India and 2A is Amul Jamnagar and Amul Pune.

Is there any standard solution for this OR what are the enhancements required to achieve this.

Someone please help me on this.

Thanks and Regards.

Saurav Mallick.

entry.title: Re: how use MDRO.SendLogViaEmail function sap c4c absl
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

The issue is likely related to the system sender configuration.

For MDRO.SendLogViaEmail() to work properly, a valid sender address must be configured, typically in the format donotreply@myXXXXX.mail.crm.ondemand.com

If the sender is not configured or enabled in the tenant, the email will not be sent even if the job finishes without errors.

I recommend checking the Outbound Email configuration in the system.

Best regards.

Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: Re: Queue Browser: Deep Message Visibility for Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi @alex_myakinkii,

Thanks for your enthusiasm about improving the developer experience in SAP Integration Suite! I can see you've put significant effort into your CPI monitoring solutions.
To clarify, the Queue Browser feature announced here is specifically for Event Mesh (the message broker capability within Integration Suite), which addresses message queue inspection and troubleshooting. This is different from the CPI Message Processing Logs (MPL) and API Management session debugging challenges you've been tackling with your custom Fiori apps.

Your feedback on CPI and APIM monitoring tools is appreciated. I have forwarded your feedback to the relevant product teams.
